cfo_summary: Total available cash is $226,469 across all nine entities. After $13,126 in approved external payments and $1,761 in intercompany transfers this week, projected end-of-week balance is $213,343. This is the strongest cash position in several weeks driven by the WW closings and CP sales landing in the bank.

GRP projects to $30,460 after receiving the CA management fee and paying $6,781 in maintenance and cleaning bills. GRP's available balance of $35,481 reflects the recovery from last week's negative position — the WW:6D and WW:18A closings appear to have funded through. Confirm with Beth that both WW closings have cleared.

Three homes are listing this week per the June 8 ops meeting — FS1:17 (application already approved), FS2:28, and FS2:29. FS1:17 is the highest probability near-term close. CP:44 Waterway and CP:2141 Patricia are both pending sale through Vanderbilt with approvals in hand.

RMF projects to $2,405 (thin) after the $3,450 Western Remodeling payment. No action needed this week but flag for next week if no deposits come in. Group Group projects to $4,378 (thin) — no payments out but no deposits in. The June Tim distribution has not appeared in this week's bills.

Open lot premiums ($21,307 total) remain on hold — CP $11,969, CA $1,140, and FS $8,198. With CP projecting to $79,951 and DA at $31,607 there is capacity to approve these. Recommend discussing in Thursday's meeting whether to approve the open lot payments now or continue holding.
No BvA or Monthly Outlook this week — P&L export not uploaded. BvA will resume next week.

for_ownership.label: Projected end-of-week cash
for_ownership.paragraph: We close this week at roughly $213,343 available across all accounts after $13,126 in external payments. This is a significant improvement from recent weeks reflecting the WW home closings and CP sales proceeds now in the bank. Three more homes are listing this week at Four Seasons and one application is already approved. The open lot insurance premiums of $21K remain on hold and will need to be approved once the cash position is confirmed stable. RMF and Group Group are the only thin entities this week.

forecast: CP:44 Waterway and CP:2141 Patricia are both pending sale through Vanderbilt with approvals in hand. Waterway is at $27K (title pending from state), and Patricia is Diana Mayo at $25K with zero down. Xochitl picked both up within her first two weeks back from leave. Both expected to close in June.

FS1:17 has an approved application as of June 8 and the financing link was sent. Expected to move to pending sale quickly at $25K. FS2:28 and FS2:29 are listing this week after final cleaning — both rehab complete. FS2:01 financing continues with Jessica Rienquist at $29K.

RE:43 demolition timeline revised — To Do Property Cleanup is not available until mid-July. Utility disconnect request to City of Wamego should go out approximately July 1. This is a cost avoidance item, not a cash inflow.

CP:9 Corey remains pending — waiting on Beth to provide the 21st Century financing code. Tenants are in the home and ready to buy. This is flagged as overdue in the June 8 action items. FS2:3 will come back as make-ready at end of June after tenant Nathaniel Foreman vacates following the burglary incident.

Open lot premiums ($21,307) and Trent distribution ($52,000) remain the two largest unfunded obligations. With the portfolio cash position improving, recommend confirming the open lot approval and Trent distribution timing in Thursday's meeting.
